nodes.label: AI as efficiency booster for quick tasks
nodes.grounding_span: if it is a very simple CSS fix or a quick one-liner that needs changed, I'd prefer just asking Copilot since it will locate the file and line faster than I will

nodes.label: AI as architectural thinking partner for complex tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable for large-scale changes without refinement
nodes.grounding_span: if we are making modifications that are more significant, like adding Server-Side Rendering for a specific page for example, I will usually think about the change on my own, ask Gemini or some other LLM from an architectural standpoint, devise a workflow, then prompt the coding agent to make an attempt

nodes.label: AI risk in version control operations
nodes.grounding_span: whenever it comes to pushing changes or dealing with GitHub I tend to ensure I am the one performing the task. This kind of task is different because I feel there is only a tiny hallucination needed to do something dreadful like pushing to main directly

nodes.label: AI understanding of codebase context
nodes.label_negative: AI lack of holistic codebase understanding
nodes.grounding_span: The bulk of my frustrations come from a lack of understanding most of the time, like not grasping the entirety of the codebase

nodes.label: AI as future junior developer replacement
nodes.grounding_span: I am almost positive the job I am currently doing is the same one that AI could do years from now. I am all but certain AI will be able to act as a Junior developer and propose solutions back to Seniors

nodes.label: AI as specification-driven autonomous agent
nodes.grounding_span: I envision a more hands-off approach where AI I am less interacting with the codebase itself and more-so devising a specifications sheet more or less that gets sent to an agent and 15-30 minutes later I see the proposed changes and can test / approve them as necessary

nodes.label: Productivity and efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: maximizing efficiency whilst still using it safely

nodes.label: Safety and risk avoidance
nodes.grounding_span: there is only a tiny hallucination needed to do something dreadful like pushing to main directly

nodes.label: Career advancement and future-proofing
nodes.grounding_span: I am actively pursuing different areas in Tech that I feel will be safer, and I am also trying to push for higher levels faster than normal to make it out of the Junior role

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for quick tasks
nodes.grounding_span: if it is a very simple CSS fix or a quick one-liner that needs changed, I'd prefer just asking Copilot since it will locate the file and line faster than I will

nodes.label: Frustration with AI's lack of codebase understanding
nodes.grounding_span: The bulk of my frustrations come from a lack of understanding most of the time, like not grasping the entirety of the codebase

nodes.label: Caution toward AI in high-risk tasks
nodes.grounding_span: whenever it comes to pushing changes or dealing with GitHub I tend to ensure I am the one performing the task

nodes.label: Optimistic vision of AI as autonomous agent
nodes.grounding_span: I envision a more hands-off approach where AI I am less interacting with the codebase itself and more-so devising a specifications sheet

nodes.label: Task triaging by complexity
nodes.grounding_span: It really depends on the type of task that it is and the amount of changes being made

nodes.label: Proactive career planning in response to AI
nodes.grounding_span: I am actively pursuing different areas in Tech that I feel will be safer, and I am also trying to push for higher levels faster than normal
